Title: Successful user registration with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- User is on the Flash activity screen.
- Network connection is stable.
- Database (Postgresol) is accessible.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Register" from the Flash activity.
2. User enters valid username, email, and password (meeting complexity requirements).
3. User confirms password.
4. User submits the registration form.
Expected Result:
- System validates inputs and accepts registration.
- User is redirected to the Login activity.
- Confirmation message appears: "Registration successful. Please log in."
- Database (Postgresol) records the new user with hashed password.

Title: Registration failure with duplicate email
Preconditions:
- User `john.doe@example.com` already exists in Postgresol.
- User is on the Flash activity screen.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Register" from the Flash activity.
2. User enters username, duplicate email, and password.
3. User submits the form.
Expected Result:
- System rejects registration.
- Error message appears: "Email already registered. Use a different email."
- User remains on the registration screen.

Title: Registration failure with weak password
Preconditions: - User is on the Flash activity screen.
Test Data:
- Username: `weak_user`
- Email: `weak@example.com`
- Password: `123` (too short and simple)
Test Steps:
1. User clicks "Register."
2. User enters username, email, and weak password.
3. User submits the form.
Expected Result:
- System rejects registration.
- Error message appears: "Password must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character."
- User remains on the registration screen.

Title: Successful login with valid credentials
Preconditions:
- User `john.doe@example.com` exists in Postgresol with password `SecurePass123!`.
- User is on the Login activity screen.
Test Steps:
1. User enters registered email and correct password.
2. User clicks "Login."
Expected Result:
- System authenticates the user.
- User is redirected to the Newsletter activity.
- Notification center receives login event (if configured).

Title: Login failure with incorrect password
Preconditions:
- User `john.doe@example.com` exists with password `SecurePass123!`.
- User is on the Login activity screen.
Test Steps:
1. User enters correct email and incorrect password.
2. User clicks "Login."
Expected Result:
- System rejects login.
- Error message appears: "Invalid email or password."
- User remains on the Login activity screen.
- No notification is triggered.

Title: Subscribe to newsletter successfully
Preconditions:
- User is logged in (from Login activity).
- Newsletter management system is active.
Test Data:
- Newsletter topic: "Tech Updates"
- User email: `john.doe@example.com` (already logged in)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to Newsletter activity.
2. User selects "Tech Updates" checkbox.
3. User submits subscription.
Expected Result:
- System confirms subscription.
- Notification center sends confirmation email to `john.doe@example.com`.
- Postgresol records the subscription.
- User sees: "Subscribed to Tech Updates. Check your email for confirmation."

Title: Unsubscribe from newsletter
Preconditions:
- User `john.doe@example.com` is subscribed to "Tech Updates."
- User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to Newsletter activity.
2. User selects "Tech Updates" and clicks "Unsubscribe."
3. User confirms action.
Expected Result:
- System removes subscription.
- Notification center sends unsubscribe confirmation email.
- Postgresol updates user record.
- User sees: "Unsubscribed from Tech Updates."

Title: Create a new event successfully
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Event creation form is accessible.
Test Data:
- Event name: "Tech Conference 2023"
- Date: `2023-12-15`
- Time: `09:00 AM`
- Location: "Online"
- Description: "Annual tech conference with keynote speakers."
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to "Create event" activity.
2. User fills in all event details.
3. User clicks "Save."
Expected Result:
- System validates and saves the event.
- Postgresol records the event with user ID.
- Notification center sends confirmation to user.
- User sees: "Event 'Tech Conference 2023' created successfully."

Title: View an existing event
Preconditions:
- Event "Tech Conference 2023" exists in Postgresol.
- User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to "Join/view event" activity.
2. User searches for "Tech Conference 2023."
3. User selects the event.
Expected Result:
- System displays event details (name, date, location, description).
- User can see "Join" or "Already Joined" button.
- No errors occur.

Title: Join an event successfully
Preconditions:
- Event "Tech Conference 2023" exists and is public.
- User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User views event details.
2. User clicks "Join."
3. User confirms action.
Expected Result:
- System records user participation in Postgresol.
- Notification center sends join confirmation to user.
- User sees: "You have joined Tech Conference 2023."

Title: Update user profile successfully
Preconditions:
- User is logged in.
- Current profile data: Name = "John Doe," Bio = "Software Engineer."
Test Data:
- New name: "Johnathan Doe"
- New bio: "Senior QA Engineer"
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to Profile activity.
2. User edits name and bio fields.
3. User clicks "Save."
Expected Result:
- System updates profile in Postgresol.
- User sees updated profile data.
- Notification center logs profile change (if configured).

Title: Profile update failure with empty bio
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to Profile activity.
2. User clears bio field and clicks "Save."
Expected Result:
- System rejects update.
- Error message appears: "Bio cannot be empty."
- Profile data remains unchanged.

Title: Receive notification for new event
Preconditions:
- User is subscribed to "Tech Updates" newsletter.
- A new event "Tech Conference 2023" is created.
- Notification center is configured to alert subscribers.
Test Steps:
1. System creates "Tech Conference 2023."
2. Notification center processes event creation.
Expected Result:
- User receives email: "New Event: Tech Conference 2023."
- Notification appears in-app (if supported).
- Postgresol logs notification sent to user.

Title: Logout and terminate session
Expected Result:
- Session is terminated in Postgresol.
- User is redirected to Flash activity.
- Notification center logs logout event.
- User sees: "You have been logged out."

Title: End-to-end flow: Register → Login → Create Event → Exit
Preconditions:
- Fresh database (Postgresol) with no users.
- Notification center is active.
Test Steps:
1. User registers with `newuser@example.com` and `Pass123!`.
2. User logs in with the same credentials.
3. User creates event "Test Event."
4. User logs out.
Expected Result:
- Registration succeeds; user exists in Postgresol.
- Login succeeds; user is authenticated.
- Event is created and recorded in Postgresol.
- Notification for event creation is sent.
- Logout terminates session; user is redirected to Flash activity.

Title: System behavior during database timeout
Preconditions:
- Postgresol is temporarily unavailable (simulated timeout).
- User is on the Login activity.
Test Steps: 1. User attempts to log in during Postgresol timeout.
Expected Result:
- System displays error: "Service unavailable. Please try again later."
- User is redirected to Flash activity.
- No session is created.
- Notification center logs the failure.

Title: Concurrent event creation by multiple users
Preconditions:
- Two users (`user1@example.com`, `user2@example.com`) are logged in.
- Both attempt to create an event with the same name simultaneously.
Test Data:
- User1: Event name = "Conference 2023"
- User2: Event name = "Conference 2023"
Test Steps:
1. User1 submits event creation.
2. User2 submits event creation at the same time.
Expected Result:
- System accepts one event and rejects the duplicate.
- Rejected user sees: "Event name already exists. Please choose another."
- Postgresol contains only one event record.
- Notification center alerts User1 of success and User2 of failure.
